matches.match_rationale: The core gap is absence of standardized, timely written communication at key milestones — acknowledgment, review-in-progress, decision, waitlist, and enrollment-next-steps. LLMs can generate personalized status update emails and letters from application data, adapting tone and content to the family's language or campus. This directly attacks the 'no standardized communication milestones' mechanism.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A template-driven LLM layer sits on top of the SIS/admissions system and auto-drafts milestone emails (received, under review, decision, waitlist, enrolled) triggered by status field changes — staff approve with one click before send.

matches.match_rationale: Poor applicant communication is fundamentally a workflow failure — nobody is responsible for triggering messages at defined checkpoints, and manual follow-through breaks down at volume. A task automation agent can monitor application status fields across campuses, detect when a milestone has been reached or a SLA breached, and autonomously trigger the appropriate communication or escalation without staff remembering to act. This addresses both the 'no standardized milestones' and 'delayed or not proactively communicated' mechanisms.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An agent continuously polls the admissions database, detects status transitions or overdue-decision flags, then orchestrates outbound communication (email/SMS), updates a family-facing portal, and logs the action — closing the loop without manual effort.

matches.match_rationale: A large share of the communication pain comes from families proactively calling or emailing to ask 'where is my application?' — consuming admissions staff time while still leaving families uncertain. A conversational agent with real-time read access to the application database can answer status queries 24/7 across web, SMS, or WhatsApp, resolving the uncertainty without staff involvement. Multi-site operators benefit especially because a single agent deployment covers all campuses.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A parent-facing chatbot (web + SMS) authenticates via application reference number and surfaces live status, next steps, and estimated timeline — deflecting the majority of inbound status calls and emails while improving family experience.

matches.match_rationale: Inbound emails and messages from families about application status are often mixed with other inquiries — document requests, fee queries, tour bookings. Text classification can triage incoming messages, flag status-inquiry messages as high-priority, and route them to the right person or trigger an automated response, reducing the lag time that creates the reputational damage families experience.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An email triage classifier tags incoming parent messages by intent (status inquiry, document submission, complaint, general) and auto-responds to status inquiries with a current-status pull from the SIS, escalating anything requiring human judgment.
